doctor_summary:
The patient has active diabetes with poor glycemic control, evidenced by an HbA1c of 8.1% and recent glucose values of 138 mg/dL and 150 mg/dL. The current diabetes regimen includes insulin nph & regular human. Poor glycemic control increases the risk for diabetes progression and complications. 
The patient is also experiencing hypertensive urgency, with a recorded blood pressure of 200/103 mmHg. This severe hypertension persists despite a complex regimen of atenolol, clonidine, furosemide, hydralazine, hydrochlorothiazide, lisinopril, metoprolol, and nifedipine. This indicates a high risk for end-organ damage and cardiovascular events, requiring prompt intervention to prevent progression to emergency.
patient_summary:
Your diabetes is active, and your blood sugar levels are high. Your HbA1c test result was 8.1%, and recent blood sugar readings were 138 and 150 mg/dL. You are currently taking insulin to manage your diabetes. 
Your blood pressure is very high at 200/103 mmHg, which is considered a hypertensive urgency. You are taking several medications for your blood pressure, including atenolol, clonidine, furosemide, hydralazine, hydrochlorothiazide, lisinopril, metoprolol, and nifedipine. 
These high blood sugar and blood pressure levels need close attention because they can lead to more serious health problems if not well-managed. Your current treatments may need to be reviewed to better control these levels.
